metadata.title: Apply for Ma Earth Funding Round 3 — Certs
metadata.description: Learn how GainForest communities can use Certs to prepare for Ma Earth Funding Round 3 and the $500,000 matching pool.

hero.title: Prepare your Cert for Ma Earth Funding Round 3
hero.description: Ma Earth is running a community-backed funding round with a $500,000 matching pool for nature-based projects. If your organization already documents impact through Certs, use your public Cert as a reference when you apply on Ma Earth.

matchingPool.label: Matching pool
matchingPool.description: Ma Earth uses quadratic funding: broader supporter participation can matter more than one large contribution.

matchingPool.label: Matching pool
matchingPool.description: Community support is amplified through Ma Earth’s matching model.

fundingRange.label: Project funding range
fundingRange.description: For specific, feasible nature-based work with clear reporting.

why.title: Your impact record should travel with your application.
why.description: A Cert gathers the project story, organization context, location, photos, and evidence that funders need to trust the work. It helps bridge verified community work to Ma Earth’s funding process.

includeItems.evidence: Photos, evidence, and documented impact claims
includeItems.location: Location or site references when available
includeItems.publicLink: A public Cert link you can include in your application

items.nature: Nature-based restoration, conservation, agriculture, water, soil, biodiversity, or community projects.
items.plan: A specific plan with goals, timeframe, reporting capacity, and authentic success claims.
items.fundingNeed: A funding need between USD $2,000 and $15,000 for project-specific work.
items.stripe: A Stripe-supported country setup or a fiscal host partner that can receive funds.

roundCloses.event: Round closes and funding is paid

flow.title: Apply directly on Ma Earth, with your Cert ready.
flow.description: Certs cannot submit your application automatically. Open the Ma Earth fundraise page, apply there, and include your public Cert link wherever it helps explain your project and evidence.

metadata.title: Explore Projects — Verified Regenerative Impact Work
metadata.description: Browse project collections from communities and organizations, each grouping the Certs that document their regenerative impact.

hero.title: Browse
hero.description: Projects group related Certs from communities and organizations, so you can browse work at the parent-project level before diving into individual certificates.
